title: Modular Robot Workshop
subtitle: Resilient embodiment, adaptive morphology, and scalable intelligence for modular robotic systems.
eventDateDisplay: October 2026 (final date to be confirmed by IROS 2026)
countdownFallback: Set a confirmed date in workshopDateIso once IROS 2026 schedule is finalized.
overview: This workshop brings together researchers and practitioners working on modular robots across design, planning, control, and learning. We focus on systems that can reconfigure morphology and behavior to handle uncertainty, varying tasks, and deployment constraints. The event aims to connect embodied intelligence research with real-world fielded platforms.
cfpIntro: We invite extended abstracts, demonstrations, and position papers on theory, systems, and applications of modular robotics. Interdisciplinary work is especially encouraged.

venueText: Travel details will be aligned with the official IROS 2026 conference information. Update this section with room assignment, accessibility notes, visa guidance, and local transit once published.

faq.q: Do I need a full paper to participate?
faq.a: No. We welcome extended abstracts, position pieces, demos, and already-published work with clear relevance to modular robotics.
The current plan is hybrid. Final instructions for remote presenters will be posted once the platform is confirmed.
Yes. We strongly encourage industrial contributions, especially real deployment case studies and open benchmark datasets.
